name: Dangerous left curve (about 150m after the sign)
Dangerous right curve (about 150m after the sign)

Series of curves, first one to the left (about 150m after the sign)
It is possible to have multiple curves to the left and none to the right
osmTags: curves=serpentine* *only add this if the curves are in different directions

name:
Series of curves, first one to the left (about 150m after the sign)
It is possible to have multiple curves to the right and none to the left
Steep descent (about 150m after the sign)
Steep ascent (about 150m after the sign)
Road narrows (about 150m after the sign)
osmTags: traffic_calming=choker on part of the way (this sign can be used for other kinds of narrowing than traffic calming)

name: Moveable bridge (about 150m after the sign)
Quay or river bank ahead (about 150m after the sign)
osmTags: water=river (Please see the respective page on how to map correctly)

name: Bumpy road (about 150m after the sign)
Speed bump (about 150m after the sign)
osmTags: traffic_calming=cushion. See wiki for more details. There is a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h on all kinds of traffic calming speed bumps, if they are indicated by signs A14 and F87, or if they are located on an intersection and indicated by sign A14. (law nl fr). Technically, it's not necesary to map this
maxspeed=* as it's part of the Belgian default relation.

name: Slippery road (about 150m after the sign)
Loose chippings (about 150m after the sign)
Falling rocks (about 150m after the sign)
Pedestrian crossing (about 150m after the sign)
Place where a lot of children come (about 150m after the sign)
Bicycle and moped crossing or place where bicyclists and mopeds exit the cycleway and enter the road (about 150m after the sign)
Wild animal crossing (about 150m after the sign)
Cattle crossing (about 150m after the sign)
Construction (about 150m after the sign)
Traffic lights (about 150m after the sign)
Low flying aircraft (about 150m after the sign)
Side wind (about 150m after the sign)
Two-way traffic after part with one-way traffic
Railway crossing with gates (about 150m after the sign)
Railway crossing without gates (about 150m after the sign)
Railway with single track crossing
Railway with two or more tracks crossing
Tram crossing (about 150m after the sign)
Queues are likely (mostly used on dynamic signs)
Caution sign. A sign under it details the danger
Give way
Give way, only applies to cyclists
Give way, only applies to bicycles and motorcycles
B1 sign ahead at given distance
Stop and give way
Stop and give way, only applies to cyclists
Stop and give way, only applies to cyclists and motorcycles
B5 sign ahead at given distance
Priority road. Not allowed to park along this road.
End of priority road
B11 sign ahead at given distance
Priority at next intersection
Intersection with priority to the right
Narrow passage, give way to traffic from opposite side
Narrow passage, priority over traffic from opposite side
Cyclists can cross the traffic lights to turn right when the traffic lights are red or amber, if they give way.
osmTags: traffic_signals=* on the road and add

name: Cyclists can cross the traffic lights to go straight on when the traffic lights are red or amber, if they give way. In rare cases, B22 and B23 can occur together
osmTags: traffic_signals=* on the road and add

name: Forbidden direction for all drivers.
osmTags: oneway=yes make sure the direction of the way is the same as the driving direction

name: No entry, in both directions, for all drivers.
osmTags: horse=no Also try to add the necessary tag when there is an exception sign. Please see Key:access for exceptions not listed below.

name: No entry for drivers of motor vehicles with more than two wheels and motorcycles with side-car
No entry for drivers of motor vehicles with four wheels, constructed for unpaved surfaces, with an open body work, handlebars like a motorcycle and a seat. (For example ATVs...)
No entry for drivers motorcycles
No entry for drivers of mopeds (and mofas)
No entry for drivers of 'rijwielen'/'cycles'. (For example bicycles, tricycles ...)
No entry for drivers of carriages
No entry for horse riders
No entry for drivers of hand-carts
No entry for pedestrians
No entry for drivers of vehicles with more mass than indicated
No entry for drivers of coaches (fr: autocars) (this sign doesn't prohibit buses used as public transport)
osmTags: coach=no (attention: bus=no is used to limit public-transport buses, and is not applicable here).

name: No entry for drivers of motor vehicles and combination vehicles designed and constructed for the transport of goods.
No entry for drivers of vehicles transporting hazardous substances
No entry for drivers of vehicles transporting inflammable or explosive substances
No entry for drivers of vehicles transporting polluting substances
No entry for drivers of vehicles or combination vehicles which are longer than indicated (including the cargo)
No entry for drivers of vehicles which are wider than indicated (including the cargo)
No entry for drivers of vehicles which are higher than indicated (including the cargo)
No turn allowed in the direction inicated by the arrow at the next intersection
No u-turn allowed until and including the next intersection
No overtaking of carriages and vehicles with more than two wheels until the next intersection
osmTags: overtaking=no if overtaking is not allowed in both directions.
overtaking=forward if overtaking is allowed when driving in the direction of the way but not in the other direction.
overtaking=backward if overtaking is allowed when driving against the direction of the way but not in the other direction.

name: End of overtaking prohibition from sign C35

No overtaking of carriages or vehicles with more than two wheels by drivers of vehicles or combination vehicles used for goods transport with maximum allowed mass over 3500kg until the next intersection
End of overtaking prohibition from sign C39

Maximum speed is as indicated until the next intersection, or until another (zonal) C43 sign, or until the sign that indicates the start or end of a built up area, living street or pedestrian zone.
When the sign C43 indicates "30" and is put above a F1 sign, the maximum speed applies to the entire built-up area.
End of speed limit from sign C43
osmTags: maxspeed=50 (within built-up areas in Flanders and Wallonia)
maxspeed=90 (outside built-up area in Wallonia), or
maxspeed=120 (on roads with at least two lanes in every direction and a central barrier)

name: End of all prohibitions imposed by other signs to all moving vehicles
Toll booths. No driving through without stopping

Not allowed to use cruise control until the next intersection
A sign with a maximum mass can be added so it only applies to vehicles with maximum allowed mass higher than indicated
End of prohibition imposed by sign C48
No entry for vehicles with more than two wheels and motorcycles
No entry for vehicles with more than two wheels, motorcycles and mopeds (class A, B and P)
No entry for 'rijwielen'/'cycles' and mopeds (class A, B and P)
Mandatory to follow the direction indicated by the arrow
Mandatory to follow one of the directions indicated the arrows
Mandatory for vehicles with dangerous goods to follow the direction indicated by the arrow
Roundabout

Mandatory cycleway (cyclists and mofas must use it. Pedestrians are allowed to use it if there is no sidewalk or verge. Mopeds are allowed to use it where speed limit is below 50 km/h. Mopeds must use it where speed limit is above 50 km/h. Speed pedelecs follow the same rules as mopeds. Other road users cannot use it)
osmTags: cycleway=track. (As a rare exception, it could be cycleway=lane.) When the cycleway is separate or needs to be separated from a highway, then use
foot=yes/no (generally "no", but "yes" if there is no sidewalk or verge)
mofa=use_sidepath on the main road. Outside of built-up areas (speed limit higher than 50 km/h) in addition tag
speed_pedelec=use_sidepath on the main road. When deciding to split off a cycleway from the main road, check "Choosing between

name: Part of the road reserved for pedestrians, cyclists and mofas (also mandatory to use it)
osmTags: cycleway:segregated=yes If this is a separate cycleway, use

segregated=yes (there are examples where this sign is used wrongly and you would need to add segregated=no instead (example)) When deciding to split off a cycleway from the main road, check "Choosing between

name: Part of the road reserved for cyclists and pedestrians (also mandatory to use it)
osmTags: segregated=no. Please see the wiki page. For more complex situation (track on one side, used in both directions etc...) If this is a separate cycleway, use
segregated=no When deciding to split off a cycleway from the main road, check "Choosing between

name: Mandatory footway (pedestrians must use it, no other road user allowed)
Mandatory part of the road for horse riders (horse riders must use it, no other road user allowed)
No parking allowed. An extra sign can indicate when the prohibition is in force.
osmTags: parking:side:restriction=no_parking (Example: parking:both:restriction=no_parking should be used when traffic sign E1 is present on both sides of the road)

name: No parking or standing still allowed. An extra sign can indicate when the prohibition is in force.
osmTags: parking:side:restriction=no_stopping (Example: parking:left:restriction=no_stopping should be used when traffic sign E3 is present on the left side of the road, seen in the direction of the way on OSM)

name: No parking allowed on this side of the road from 1st day of the month until the 15th
osmTags: parking:side:restriction:conditional=no_parking @ (Jan 01-15,Feb 01-15,Mar 01-15,Apr 01-15,May 01-15,Jun 01-15,Jul 01-15,Aug 01-15,Sep 01-15,Oct 01-15,Nov 01-15,Dec 01-15) where side is typically either left or right.

name: No parking allowed on this side of the road from the 16th day of the month until the last
osmTags: parking:side:restriction:conditional=no_parking @ (Jan 16-31,Feb 16-29,Mar 16-31,Apr 16-30,May 16-31,Jun 16-30,Jul 16-31,Aug 16-31,Sep 16-30,Oct 16-31,Nov 16-30,Dec 16-31) where side is typically either left or right.

name: Parking allowed
osmTags: amenity=parking to the separate area. Add
parking:side=separate to the street centre line. When tagging as part of a highway

parking:side=* where * will be lane (parking spaces on the street, could be easily converted to a travel lane) or street_side (parking spaces in parking bays adjacent to the road, cannot be easily converted to a travel lane) or shoulder (parking spaces on the shoulder: hard, paved or unpaved sections beside the road not normally meant to be driven on).

name: Parking exclusively for motorcycles, motorcars and minibuses
osmTags: motorcar=yes to the separate area. Add
parking:side=separate to the street centre line. When tagging as part of a highway, add
parking:side:motorcar=yes to the street centre line.

name: Parking exclusively for lorries
osmTags: hgv=yes to the separate area. Add
parking:side=separate to the street centre line. When tagging as part of a highway, add
parking:side:hgv=yes to the street centre line.

name: Parking exclusively for coaches and tourist buses (NL: autocars)
osmTags: tourist_bus=yes to the separate area. Add
parking:side=separate to the street centre line. When tagging as part of a highway, add
parking:side:tourist_bus=yes to the street centre line.

name: Parking mandatory on pavement or verge
osmTags: parking:side=on_kerb When tagging as an area, add
orientation=parallel to the separate area. Add
parking:side=separate to the street centre line.

name: Parking mandatory partly on pavement or verge
osmTags: parking:side=half_on_kerb When tagging as an area, add
orientation=parallel to the separate area. Add
parking:side=separate to the street centre line.

name: Parking mandatory on the roadway
osmTags: parking:side=lane When tagging as an area, add
orientation=parallel to the separate area. Add
parking:side=separate to the street centre line.

name: Parking exclusively for motorhomes
osmTags: parking:side:motorhome=yes to the street centre line. When tagging as an area, add
motorhome=yes to the separate area. Add
parking:side=separate to the street centre line.

name: Parking exclusively for motorcycles
osmTags: parking:side:motorcycle=yes to the street centre line. When tagging as an area, add
motorcycle=yes to the separate area. Add
parking:side=separate to the street centre line. Alternatively, the tag

name: parking alternates over time between different vehicle classes
osmTags: :conditional=designated @ (Mo-Su *) to the street centre line with * being the vehicle class (applied to the example given: parking:side:bicycle:conditional=designated @ (Mo-Su 08:00-17:30) and parking:side:motorcar:conditional=designated @ (Mo-Su 17:30-08:00)) to the street centre line. When tagging as an area, add

:conditional=designated @ (Mo-Su *) to the street centre line with * being the vehicle class (applied to the example given: bicycle:conditional=designated @ (Mo-Su 08:00-17:30) and motorcar:conditional=designated @ (Mo-Su 17:30-08:00)) to the separate area. Add
parking:side=separate to the street centre line.

name: Is put above an F1* sign. Within the whole built-up area, parking only allowed on the side of the road with odd-numbered houses between days 1 and 15 of the month, even numbers otherwise.
osmTags: parking:side:restriction:reason=BE:E11 could be used. It aligns with the meaning of the
parking:side:restriction:reason=* key, but is undocumented at this time.

Area where cyclists and drivers of mopeds can wait at an intersection with traffic lights
Indicating the direction you're heading to if you follow the lanes below the arrows

Indicating a lane reserved for buses

Indicating lanes reserved for public transport vehicles
Road with one-way traffic

oneway=-1 Also try to always tag
oneway:speed_pedelec=* depending on the possible exception sign

osmTags: destination:backward=*, depending on the direction of the way.

name: Sign showing possible directions
Arrow indicating direction of a place (village, city...)
Arrow indicating direction of a place. You'll have to follow a motorway.
Arrow indicating direction of a place (airport, port, industrial area...)
Arrow indicating direction of a touristic valley or waterway
Arrow indicating direction of a place (touristic location, sport centre...)
Arrow indicating direction of a place (station, police, cemetery, post office...)
Arrow indicating direction of a place for indicated categories of vehicles

Arrow indicating direction of a touristic location for indicated categories of vehicles
Arrow indicating direction of a touristic location

Sign announcing diversion
Arrow indicating direction to follow for the diversion
Municipality border
No exit
osmTags: noexit=yes on the way itself. If you want, you could add
noexit=yes on the last node of the way, as described on the tag page. Actually, it's only used to inform other mappers the road is really ending, and it's not a mapping mistake.

name: No exit, except pedestrians and bicycles
osmTags: noexit=yes on the way itself. If you want, you could add

noexit=yes on the last node of the way, as described on the tag page. Actually, it's only used to inform other mappers the road is really ending, and it's not a mapping mistake. You can add the correct access tags or highway type on the part of the road where pedestrians and bicycles can pass.

Elevated pedestrian crossing

Indicating roofed over parking

name: Camp site
Caravan site
Youth hostel
Touristic information
Temporary road works sign indicating lane decrease
Temporary road works sign indicating lane deviation
Temporary road works sign indicating crossing of median on dual carriageway
Temporary road works sign indicating two-way traffic on road which is usually one-way only
Speed bump
osmTags: traffic_calming=cushion. See wiki for more details. There is a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h on all kinds of traffic calming speed bumps, if they are indicated by signs A14 and F87, or if they are located on an intersection and indicated by sign A14. maxspeed=* as it's part of the the Belgian default relation.

name: Announces rules or dangers on specific lanes only
Indicates rules or dangers on specific lanes only
Indicates radio broadcasting service that gives traffic information
"Noodstopstrook"/"Piste de détresse": emergency stop strip (where you can stop when brakes don't work during a descent)
Indicates lane decrease
"Vluchthaven"/"Garage": where you can stop along the road in case of emergency
osmTags: highway=emergency_bay Until recently, unofficial sign IF2 was also used to indicate emergency bays outside of tunnels. These are all being replaced with F98.

name: Road reserved for pedestrians, speed pedelecs, bicycles, and horses (vehicles only have access by exception: emergency vehicles and vehicles with written permission)

Road reserved for pedestrian and bicycles on indicated part of the road (vehicles only have access by exception: emergency vehicles and vehicles with written permission)
Can be adapted according to different categories
Road reserved for agriculture vehicles, speed pedelecs, pedestrians, horses and bicycles (emergency vehicles and local traffic allowed). Can be adapted according to different categories.

End of road reserved for pedestrians, bicycles and horses
Can be adapted according to different categories
End of road reserved for pedestrian and bicycles on indicated part of the road.
End of road reserved for agriculture vehicles, speed pedelecs, pedestrians, bicycles and horses.

Pedestrian area.
Only pedestrians are allowed. If there is a bicycle on the sign, bicycles are also allowed. Permit holders are also allowed. Delivery vehicles and taxis may also be allowed when mentioned on the sign. Access to delivery vehicles, taxis and bicyclists may be restricted to certain time periods and days, as mentioned on the sign.
osmTags: bus=yes Examples of possible restrictions added to the sign: Bicycles always allowed (sign has bicycle icon, without hour restriction):
vehicle:conditional=yes @ delivery Loading/unloading only allowed between 18h30 and 11h00:
vehicle:conditional=delivery @ (18:30-11:00) (adjust accordingly) Loading/unloading only allowed for vehicles less than 10 ton between 18h30 and 11h00:
vehicle:conditional=delivery @ (18:30-11:00 AND weight<10) (adjust accordingly) Taxis always allowed:
taxi=destination Taxis only allowed between 18h30 and 11h00:
taxi:conditional=destination @ (18:30-11:00) Other conditional restrictions might be possible

name: End of pedestrian area
Doesn't exist anymore
Doesn't exist anymore

Start of road dedicated to bicycle traffic untill sign F113 or until the next junction.
This sign was removed from the Wegcode on 1 April 2023. 
The signs have to be replaced by the ones below by 1 January 2035.

This sign was removed from the Wegcode on 1 April 2023. 
The signs can still be kept untill 1 January 2032.

Traffic signs lose their meaning ZF111 with "Zone" validity or stating "Fietsstraat" Replaced by R17  on 01/01/2035 wegcode.be

This sign was removed from the Wegcode on 1 April 2023. 
The signs can still be kept untill 1 January 2032.

name:
End of road dedicated to bicycle traffic

The signs have to be replaced by the ones below by 1 January 2035.

name: Start of a Low Emission Zone (LEZ).
osmTags: boundary=low_emission_zone. Currently, the ones in Brussels, Antwerp and Ghent are already tagged. Please see this wiki page before adding any new.

name: End of a Low Emission Zone (LEZ).
osmTags: boundary=low_emission_zone. Currently, the ones in Brussels, Antwerp and Ghent are already tagged. Please see this wiki page before adding any new.

name: Start of an airport area.
End of an airport area.
Placed under sign B1, B5 or E9a when that sign only applies to bicycles.

(French text "EXCEPTÉ") (German text "AUSSER")
Placed under sign C1, C3, C31, D1 or D3 when that sign doesn't apply to bicycles.
osmTags: bicycle=yes under C31, D1 or D3:
except=bicycle in the restriction relation other signs: see M4

name:
(French text "EXCEPTÉ") (German text "AUSSER")
Placed under sign C1, C3, C31, D1 or D3 when that sign doesn't apply to bicycles and mopeds class A.
osmTags: mofa=yes under C31, D1 or D3:
except=bicycle;mofa in the restriction relation other signs: see M5

name:
(French text "EXCEPTÉ") (German text "AUSSER")
Placed under sign C1, C3, C31, D1 or D3 when that sign doesn't apply to bicycles and all mopeds (classes A, B and P).
osmTags: speed_pedelec=yes under C31, D1 or D3:
except=bicycle;mofa;moped;speed_pedelec in the restriction relation other signs: see M5bis

name: Placed under sign F19 when cyclists are allowed to drive in both directions on a one-way road
Placed under sign F19 when cyclists and mopeds class A are allowed to drive in both directions on a one-way road

(French text "OBLIGATOIRE")
Placed under sign D7 when mopeds class B have to drive on the cycleway.

(French text "INTERDIT")
Placed under sign D7 when mopeds class B are not allowed to drive on the cycleway.
Placed under sign B1, B5 or E9a when that sign only applies to bicycles and mopeds.

Placed under sign B1, B5 or B17 when bicycles can come from both directions at the intersection. 
(For example when crossing a two-way cycle lane when entering the intersection, or when bicycles are allowed to drive in both directions on a one-way road.)

Placed under sign B1, B5 or B17 when bicycles, mopeds and/or speed pedelecs can come from both directions at the intersection.
(For example when crossing a two-way cycle lane when entering the intersection, or when bicycles, mopeds and/or speed pedelecs are allowed to drive in both directions on a one-way road.)

(French text "EXCEPTÉ") (German text "AUSSER")
Placed under sign C1, C3, C31 or D1 when that sign doesn't apply to bicycles and speed pedelecs.

(French text "EXCEPTÉ") (German text "AUSSER")
Placed under sign C1, C3, C31 or D1 when that sign doesn't apply to bicycles, mopeds class A and speed pedelecs.

(French text "OBLIGATOIRE")
Placed under sign D7 when speed pedelecs have to drive on the cycleway.

(French text "OBLIGATOIRE")
Placed under sign D7 when mopeds class B and speed pedelecs have to drive on the cycleway.

(French text "INTERDIT")
Placed under sign D7 when speed pedelecs are not allowed to drive on the cycleway.

(French text "INTERDIT")
Placed under sign D7 when mopeds class B and speed pedelecs are not allowed to drive on the cycleway.
Placed under sign F19 when cyclists and speed pedelecs are allowed to drive in both directions on a one-way road.
Placed under sign F19 when cyclists, mopeds class A and speed pedelecs are allowed to drive in both directions on a one-way road.
Placed under sign B1, B5 or E9a when that sign only applies to speed pedelecs.
Placed under sign B1, B5 or E9a when that sign only applies to bicycles and speed pedelecs.
Placed under sign E9a when that sign only applies to (non-)motorised personal transporters ('voortbewegingstoestellen'/'engins de déplacement').
Placed under sign E9a when that sign only applies to shared (non-)motorised personal transporters ('deelvoortbewegingstoestellen'/'engins de déplacement en libre-partage').
Placed under sign E9a when that sign only applies to bike-share bicycles ('deelfietsen'/'bicyclettes en libre-partage').

Placed under sign E9a when that sign only applies to bike-share bicycles and (non-)motorised personal transporters ('deelfietsen en deelvoortbewegingstoestellen'/'bicyclettes et engins de déplacement en libre-partage').
